Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

Coast-To-Coast In 3 Hours: How The New Supersonic Bill Could Slash US Flight Times

Supersonic jets could revolutionary coast-to-coast travel in the US.

Regulatory approval timelines for supersonic aircraft will determine whether sub-3-hour US transcontinental flights become viable before competing international operators establish market dominance.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 26, 2026

The Top 5 Largest US Navy Supercarriers By Active Aircraft Capacity

Discover the 5 largest US Navy supercarriers by aircraft capacity, led by USS Gerald R. Ford with unmatched airpower and next-gen technology.

Modern supercarriers like USS Gerald R. Ford integrate advanced automation and digital systems that fundamentally reshape naval aviation operations and maintenance workflows compared to legacy carriers.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

A Hot Start to Spring in the Southwest

Temperatures in several states climbed over 100 degrees Fahrenheit in March 2026, which in some places was the highest March temperature on record.

Record early-season heat in the Southwest increases aircraft weight and balance constraints, reducing payload capacity and takeoff performance during peak spring travel demand.

See NASA →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

Air Canada Crash Survivors Still Without Belongings Until Crews Move Aircraft To Hangar

The survivors of AC8646 remain empty-handed.

Aircraft wreckage removal logistics create prolonged passenger hardship, highlighting the need for expedited recovery protocols in post-accident situations.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 26, 2026

Why The World’s Most-Produced Fighter Jet May Finally Disappear In 2026

Why the last combat-worthy MiG-21s likely disappeared in 2025, with only on-paper rusting hulks in service in 2026.

The MiG-21's operational retirement signals a generational shift in global air forces, as fourth-generation fighters finally replace a platform that dominated Cold War doctrine for over five decades.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective risk controls require organizations to document why residual risks are acceptable rather than simply implementing available mitigations, shifting safety culture from reactivity to intentional risk acceptance.

What goes on behind the scenes?

Pressurized belly compartments, avionics bays, and galley prep areas represent critical functional zones requiring specialized maintenance protocols that directly impact dispatch reliability and crew efficiency.

La NASA anuncia la cobertura de la misión lunar Artemis II

Nota del editor: La NASA actualizará continuamente esta página de sesiones informativas y eventos de la misión Artemis II a lo largo de las actividades previas al lanzamiento, el lanzamiento y las operaciones de la misión. La NASA ofrecerá cobertura en vivo de los eventos previos al lanzamiento, el...

NASA's structured media coverage plan for Artemis II demonstrates the agency's commitment to public transparency throughout the lunar mission lifecycle from pre-launch through operations.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Ames Experts Available for Artemis II Flight Test Interviews

NASA’s Ames Research Center in Silicon Valley invites media to interview local subject matter experts on Friday, March 27 from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m. ahead of the agency sending astronauts around the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years with the Artemis II flight test. NASA teams are gearing up...

NASA Ames is positioning its Silicon Valley research center as a key technical authority for Artemis II media coverage, signaling the critical role of specialized expertise centers in lunar exploration communications.

See NASA →

5 Airlines With The Largest Widebody Fleets In North America

All five are based in the United States.

U.S. carriers' dominance in widebody aircraft reflects their control over lucrative long-haul international routes and competitive advantages in securing financing for expensive aircraft.

How to Assess Safety Risk

A practical walkthrough of safety risk assessment under 14 CFR Part 5.

Regulatory compliance requires systematic risk assessment methodology rather than intuitive judgment alone, demanding structured documentation of safety hazards.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Sets Coverage for First Artemis Crewed Mission Around Moon

A variety of prelaunch, launch, and mission events for NASA’s Artemis II mission around the Moon will stream online. The agency is targeting no earlier than Wednesday, April 1, for the test flight during a two-hour window that opens at 6:24 p.m. EDT, with additional launch opportunities through...

NASA's April 2025 Artemis II launch window represents a critical validation of human deep-space capability before lunar surface missions, with extensive public streaming enabling transparent oversight of this flagship exploration program.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Space Force weighs launch alternatives as Vulcan faces potential months-long grounding

Officials explore shifting missions, extending satellites already in orbit

Extended satellite lifecycles and mission flexibility will become critical operational strategies if heavy-lift launch capacity remains constrained for extended periods.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Delta Air Lines Continues Los Angeles Lead, Schedules More Flights To Florida This Winter

Delta is adding more Florida flying from LAX as its battle with United intensifies.

Delta's targeted capacity expansion to Florida from LAX signals aggressive market share competition in leisure routes, particularly as carriers compete for winter travel demand from West Coast passengers.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

JetBlue Considers Selling Itself To A Major US Carrier, Weighs How Washington Will React

JetBlue's exploration of a potential sale to rival airlines has sparked intense speculation and antitrust scrutiny.

JetBlue's sale deliberations reveal how regulatory uncertainty now drives merger strategy more than operational synergies for US carriers.

See Simple Flying →

Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

FAA Part 5 SMS for Small Operators: Safety Risk Management

This video covers the safety management system (SMS) component safety risk management, which determines the need for, and adequacy of, new or revised risk controls based on the assessment of acceptable risk.

Small operators must now systematically assess and control safety risks rather than relying on reactive incident responses, fundamentally shifting compliance from checklist-based to proactive risk management.

See NBAA (YouTube) →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Up To 22-Hour Flights: United Airlines' 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Discover the airline's most extended nonstop and one-stop services, some of which are quite controversial.

Ultra-long routes demand specialized aircraft economics and crew scheduling, forcing United to optimize fuel efficiency and cabin crew rotation strategies beyond traditional network planning.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

The US Airlines With The Most Spacious Premium Economy Seats In 2026

This article will provide the reader with an in-depth look at the US airlines with the most spacious Premium Economy Seats In 2026.

US carriers are expanding premium economy as a revenue bridge between economy and business class, capitalizing on leisure travelers' willingness to pay 30-50% more for enhanced comfort.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Sets Coverage for Artemis II Moon Mission

Editor’s note: NASA will continuously update this Artemis II briefings and mission events page throughout prelaunch, launch, and mission activities. NASA will provide live coverage of prelaunch, launch, and mission events for the agency’s upcoming Artemis II crewed test flight around the Moon....

NASA's comprehensive live coverage strategy for Artemis II demonstrates the agency's commitment to public transparency in lunar exploration, which could influence how commercial spaceflight operators approach mission communications.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Artemis Moon Tree Dedicated in Honor of Mary W. Jackson

On March 18, 2026, students, staff, and members of NASA’s Langley Research Center gathered at Mary W. Jackson Elementary School in Hampton to celebrate the dedication of a remarkable addition to the campus – an Artemis Moon Tree. Although formally dedicated on this day, the loblolly pine had...

NASA's Artemis program is leveraging symbolic gestures like moon-orbited tree seeds to inspire STEM education at elementary schools, connecting space exploration milestones to community engagement and workforce development.

See NASA →

▶20:47
Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

FAA Part 5 SMS for Small Operators: Safety Policy & Safety Promotion

This video provides an overview of implementing a safety management system (SMS), specifically aligned with FAA Part 5 regulations. Join us as we explore Blue Skies Charter, a fictional company, and learn about general SMS requirements, safety policy development and safety promotion strategies. This video covers essential components like leadership commitment, gap analysis, safety objectives, emergency response planning and employee training. Whether you're a small charter operator or interested in aviation safety, this video offers valuable insights into building an effective SMS.

Small charter operators can leverage SMS implementation to systematically identify and mitigate risks through structured safety policies and employee engagement rather than reactive compliance.

See NBAA (YouTube) →

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

US House passes bill to clear way for overland supersonic flights

The US House of Representatives passed legislation aimed at opening the skies to civil supersonic flight over land,…

Overland supersonic flight authorization could unlock a significant new revenue stream for airlines while reshaping domestic route economics and passenger expectations for premium travel.

See AeroTime Hub →

eVTOL Mar 25, 2026

Eve flies eVTOL prototype for Brazilian president in high-profile test milestone

Eve Air Mobility flew its full-scale engineering prototype for Brazilian authorities, including President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva,…

Eve's demonstration to Brazil's president signals government-level confidence in eVTOL viability and may accelerate regulatory pathways for urban air mobility in Latin America.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Names Jennifer Lyons Acting Launch Services Program Manager

NASA has selected Jennifer Lyons as acting program manager for the agency’s Launch Services Program (LSP) based at Kennedy Space Center in Florida. In this role, Lyons will lead NASA’s acquisition and management of domestic commercial launch services for science and robotic exploration missions...

NASA's LSP leadership transition signals continued reliance on commercial launch providers for non-crewed science missions, directly impacting aerospace contractor competition for government contracts.

See NASA →

Drones Mar 25, 2026

BRINC Unveils Guardian, Launching the Next Era of Drone as First Responder

Guardian delivers 24/7 operations, unmatched imaging, Starlink connectivity, and more to help public safety agencies

First responders now gain persistent aerial surveillance capability through Guardian's continuous operation model, fundamentally shifting how agencies deploy drones from tactical response tools to strategic monitoring assets.

See sUAS News →

Industry Mar 26, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all aviation touchpoints—not just aircraft technology—offers the most scalable near-term pathway to meeting environmental mandates while accommodating projected traffic growth.

See MDPI →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Office of Space Commerce releases mission authorization proposal

The Office of Space Commerce has rolled out its proposal for a “light touch” approach to mission authorization, the latest attempt in an effort to regulate new commercial space applications.

The 'light touch' authorization framework could accelerate commercial space operations by reducing regulatory burden, potentially creating new market opportunities for aerospace companies seeking space access.

See SpaceNews →

Cheap drones are no longer a compromise — they are becoming the strategy.The demand is

Budget drones are evolving from entry-level alternatives into strategic tools, signaling market consolidation around cost-efficient models rather than premium features.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

NAVI AI Launches Pilot Training Platform

Navi AI has unveiled a new AI-driven platform designed to enhance pilot training by automating post-flight debriefs.

Automating post-flight debriefs reduces instructor workload and accelerates training efficiency, potentially shortening time-to-rating for commercial pilot candidates.

See AVweb →

General Aviation Mar 25, 2026

Textron Aviation To Showcase Full Range Of Aircraft At Sun ’n Fun

Textron Aviation will display Cessna, Beechcraft, and Pipistrel aircraft together for the first time at Sun ’n Fun 2026.

Textron's unified display strategy signals a shift toward cross-brand portfolio marketing to demonstrate complementary product capabilities across general aviation segments.

See AVweb →

General Aviation Mar 25, 2026

Cirrus posts strong 2025 with 691 SR Series deliveries, 106 Vision Jets

Cirrus Aircraft reported it delivered 691 SR Series piston aircraft and a record 106 Vision Jets in 2025,…

Cirrus's 106 Vision Jet deliveries demonstrate accelerating turboprop demand as single-pilot IFR aircraft increasingly compete with traditional piston markets.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Webb Captures Saturn in Infrared

Saturn’s icy rings glow in this infrared view from NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope released on March 25, 2026. Combining this image with another that NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope captured – also released on the same day – provides scientists with a richer, more layered understanding of the gas...

Webb's infrared Saturn imagery demonstrates how complementary space telescope datasets enhance planetary science research capabilities beyond single-instrument observations.

See NASA →

General Aviation Mar 25, 2026

Study Unveils Redevelopment Concepts For Burke Lakefront Airport

A new economic study outlines two potential mixed-use redevelopment plans for Burke Lakefront Airport, part of Mayor Justin Bibb’s effort to close the city-operated airport.

Cleveland's potential airport closure reflects a broader trend of underutilized urban airports transitioning to mixed-use development as cities prioritize real estate value over aviation infrastructure.

See AVweb →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Terran Orbital Introduces New Star Tracker Product Line at SATSHOW 2026

IRVINE, Calif., March 25, 2026 — Terran Orbital Corporation, a Lockheed Martin company, today announced the introduction of its new star tracker product line at SATSHOW 2026, expanding the company’s […]

Terran Orbital's new star tracker line strengthens Lockheed Martin's satellite guidance capabilities, addressing growing demand for precision navigation in the expanding commercial space sector.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Webb, Hubble Share Most Comprehensive View of Saturn to Date

NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope and Hubble Space Telescope have teamed up to capture new views of Saturn, revealing the planet in strikingly different ways. Observing in complementary wavelengths of light, the two space observatories provide scientists with a richer, more layered understanding of...

Multi-wavelength space telescope observations enhance planetary atmospheric analysis capabilities relevant to remote sensing technology development for Earth observation.
